L1.area: API controller layer handling content and file management

L2.description: A file upload vulnerability exists where the server-side file type validation can be bypassed, allowing an attacker to upload and execute dangerous file types. The insufficient extension check uses string matching combined with HTML tag stripping, which fails to adequately restrict uploads of executable files. Uploaded files are stored in a web-accessible directory with direct URL access, enabling remote code execution and server privilege escalation.

L3.area: Page content API controller and its file upload endpoints
L3.description: A file upload vulnerability exists in the image and attachment upload endpoints where the server-side file type validation can be bypassed. The extension check uses string matching combined with HTML tag stripping, which fails to adequately restrict uploads of executable files. For non-image attachment uploads, no file extension whitelist is enforced at all. Uploaded files are stored in a web-accessible directory with direct URL access, enabling remote code execution and server privilege escalation.

ground_truth.reason: The file upload endpoints in Showdoc use an insufficient file extension check (strstr with strip_tags) that can be bypassed, and the general file upload endpoint lacks any file type whitelist, allowing attackers to upload executable files to a web-accessible directory and gain server privileges.
